description: MCP client configuration for Vertex AI Memory Bank MCP Server deployed on Google Cloud Run

claude_desktop_config.comment: Add this to Claude Desktop MCP configuration

python_client_example.comment: Python client example using MCP SDK
python_client_example.code:
from mcp.client.sse import sse_client
from mcp import ClientSession

async def connect_to_cloud_run():
    async with sse_client(
        url="https://vertex-memory-bank-mcp-xxxxx-uc.a.run.app/sse"
    ) as (read, write):
        async with ClientSession(read, write) as session:
            # Initialize the session
            await session.initialize()
            
            # List available tools
            tools = await session.list_tools()
            print(f"Available tools: {tools}")
            
            # Call a tool
            result = await session.call_tool(
                "initialize_memory_bank",
                {
                    "project_id": "directed-asset-479716-f6",
                    "location": "us-central1"
                }
            )
            print(f"Result: {result}")

authentication.note: If authentication is enabled on Cloud Run, you'll need to include an Authorization header
authentication.getting_token:
Use gcloud to get an identity token:
gcloud auth print-identity-token

endpoints./health: Health check endpoint - returns service status
endpoints./sse: SSE endpoint for MCP protocol - use for streaming responses
endpoints./message: Direct JSON-RPC endpoint - use for single request/response

replacing_service_url.note: Replace 'xxxxx' in the URL with your actual Cloud Run service URL
replacing_service_url.command: gcloud run services describe vertex-memory-bank-mcp --region us-central1 --project directed-asset-479716-f6 --format 'value(status.url)'
